chunk_summary: Structured lifestyle prescriptions for pediatric MASLD in India emphasize sustainable hypocaloric diets, physical activity, and measurable follow-up targets to manage liver disease effectively, with weight maintenance and height gain as early goals for children.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of structured lifestyle prescriptions in managing pediatric MASLD in India.
Learn dietary and activity guidelines tailored for pediatric MASLD, including hypocaloric diets and physical activity targets.
Identify measurable follow-up metrics for assessing progress in pediatric MASLD management.
key_facts: Weight reduction of 3–5% can improve steatosis, while 7–10% may improve histologic features of NASH.
Early targets for children include weight maintenance with height gain; adolescents with obesity may aim for gradual weight reduction.
Diet recommendations include removing sugary drinks, reducing refined carbohydrates, increasing vegetables and fiber, and protecting adequate protein intake.
Activity prescription includes at least 60 minutes/day of moderate-to-vigorous activity or 150–200 minutes/week across 3–5 sessions.
Follow-up should reassess BMI percentile, activity minutes/week, sugary drink count/week, and ALT levels every 8–12 weeks.
reference_quote: Indian pediatric guidance supports using any sustainable hypocaloric diet pattern, with explicit avoidance of processed/junk/high-calorie foods and sugar-sweetened beverages.
document_key_topics: Structured lifestyle prescriptions for pediatric MASLD
Dietary and activity guidelines for pediatric liver disease
